```json
{
  "weights": {

  "active_set": [
    "R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.6", "R1.7",
    "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"
  ],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section foregrounds a contribution claim — that the selection-based aggregator departs from synthesis-based ensembling — and a review must assess whether that thesis is coherently and accurately framed.",
    "R1.2": "The pipeline is described as a multi-stage logical chain (detector ensemble → NMS at IoU 0.3 → learned selector → top-K calibration). Internal consistency of each step's rationale and handoff to the next is load-bearing for any review.",
    "R1.3": "An explicit quantitative claim is made (sensitivity 0.91 at 1 FP/scan described as 'competitive' with Setio benchmark numbers) without the evaluative evidence being fully unpacked; aligning that claim to the experimental basis is load-bearing.",
    "R1.4": "The section explicitly frames the selection stage as a departure from dominant FROC-optimized ensembles (Setio et al.), making novelty assessment a central, though not fully load-bearing, responsibility.",
    "R1.6": "The section exhibits recurrent writing issues: run-on sentences across compound clauses, overuse of past-perfect constructions ('we had obtained', 'we had then aggregated'), and paragraph-length sentences that impede readability.",
    "R1.7": "Multiple named references are invoked (Armato et al., Setio benchmark, LIDC-IDRI, LUNA16, NLST) with attribution claims and benchmark comparisons; accuracy and coverage of this scholarship is central to the review."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should this review cover scientific and technical content — including the validity of claims, evidence alignment, and novelty framing — or is the primary goal a writing and editorial review focused on clarity and style?"
}
```

rationale.R1.1: The section foregrounds a contribution claim — that the selection-based aggregator departs from synthesis-based ensembling — and a review must assess whether that thesis is coherently and accurately framed.
rationale.R1.2: The pipeline is described as a multi-stage logical chain (detector ensemble → NMS at IoU 0.3 → learned selector → top-K calibration). Internal consistency of each step's rationale and handoff to the next is load-bearing for any review.
rationale.R1.3: An explicit quantitative claim is made (sensitivity 0.91 at 1 FP/scan described as 'competitive' with Setio benchmark numbers) without the evaluative evidence being fully unpacked; aligning that claim to the experimental basis is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.4: The section explicitly frames the selection stage as a departure from dominant FROC-optimized ensembles (Setio et al.), making novelty assessment a central, though not fully load-bearing, responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: The section exhibits recurrent writing issues: run-on sentences across compound clauses, overuse of past-perfect constructions ('we had obtained', 'we had then aggregated'), and paragraph-length sentences that impede readability.
rationale.R1.7: Multiple named references are invoked (Armato et al., Setio benchmark, LIDC-IDRI, LUNA16, NLST) with attribution claims and benchmark comparisons; accuracy and coverage of this scholarship is central to the review.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review cover scientific and technical content — including the validity of claims, evidence alignment, and novelty framing — or is the primary goal a writing and editorial review focused on clarity and style?
